reasoning: Task appears to require a concise summary of the provided article about Andy Murray's 2017 season and Wimbledon prospects.
output: Andy Murray's 2017 season looked promising early (21–9 in his first 30 matches) but has been hampered by injuries—shingles, an elbow problem, and a troublesome left hip. After finishing 2016 as world No. 1 by winning the final events in London, he has suffered losses to players outside the top 20 (notably Jordan Thompson at Queen's) and several straight-set defeats (Fognini, Coric, Pospisil). He skipped some exhibitions but hoped grass would suit him at Wimbledon, though he returned to practice with a limp. In Roland Garros, he started slowly but beat Del Potro in straight sets in the third round, coming within a tie-break of a final before fatigue from a lack of matches curbed him in the semi-final against Wawrinka. The article argues that despite past Wimbledon success and Olympic gold, Murray is only the seventh-best in 2017 so far, struggling to impose himself and to trust his serve, with first-serve win rates down (72% from 76%) and break-point conversion down (54% from 66%). The world No. 1 race is slipping behind Nadal, with Nadal or Djokovic likely to take the top spot after Wimbledon; Federer, meanwhile, remains in form after Halle. In short, Murray remains a former champion facing ongoing injury concerns and uncertain form entering Wimbledon.
